items.description: Large ornate calligraphic initial with multiple curved flourishes and a long sweeping tail extending left into the margin.
items.scholarly_note: Decorated initial marking the beginning of a verse or section.

items.description: Prominent enlarged capital with elongated vertical stroke, loops, and decorative extensions into the margin.
items.scholarly_note: Typical large initial for poetic divisions in Codex Regius.

items.description: Large initial with long descending tail and additional curved strokes extending into margin space.
Large irregular dark brown stain covering much of the lower right area, causing significant text fading and parchment discoloration.
Scattered smaller brownish foxing stains; pronounced darkening and staining along the entire top edge.
Faint but continuous horizontal abrasion or crease traversing multiple lines in the lower text block.
Patches of surface rubbing and scratching with partial loss of ink and parchment surface.
Irregularly worn and frayed edges with small losses of parchment, especially at bottom corners and right edge.
Torn and rounded top right corner with minor parchment loss and edge wear.
Faint curved or stray ink mark at the foot of the page, distinct from the main text.

scratches: Horizontal linear abrasion/crease through lower text lines
Localized surface rubbing and ink loss in lower-right stained zone
Minor vertical scrapes adjacent to left-margin initials
damage_notes: Worn, irregular edges with corner losses at top right and bottom
Heavy staining with associated ink fading and surface abrasion in lower right
General parchment yellowing, thinning, and edge curling from age and handling
Top edge darkened with accumulated discoloration and minor tattering
summary: Folio shows three large decorated initials with flourishes extending into the left margin. Minimal textual marginalia or doodles. Prominent large stain and associated abrasion in lower right with text loss. Moderate edge wear and losses. No major scribal corrections or extensive pen trials observed beyond stray marks. Line numbers on right are modern additions. Overall condition typical for 13th-century parchment with text largely legible outside stained areas.
